sense.definition: generally perceived to exist or be the case despite being uncertain
used to say that something is true according to what people say, although it is uncertain

examples.x: The castle is reputedly haunted by the ghost of a former king.
The mysterious island is reputedly home to hidden treasures.
The artist is reputedly inspired by the landscapes of his homeland.
The manuscript is reputedly worth a small fortune.
She is reputedly the most skilled violinist in the orchestra.

sense.definition: to make something, particularly a belief or idea, less certain

examples.x: The new evidence presented in court threatened to shake the foundations of the theory.
A series of conflicting reports began to shake her confidence in the accuracy of the news.
The unexpected turn of events started to shake the team's belief in their strategy.
The criticism from experts began to shake the credibility of the research findings.
The shocking revelation about the leader's actions started to shake the loyalty of his followers.

sense.definition: uncertain about the exact details of something
used to indicate a degree of expectation regarding something that is likely to happen

examples.x: You should expect delays during rush hour traffic.
He should receive the package by the end of the week.
The company should announce their decision by tomorrow.
She should pass the exam, considering how well she studied.
We should see improvements in sales after implementing the new marketing strategy.

sense.definition: in a way or by some method that is not known or certain

examples.x: Somehow, she managed to find her way home in the dark.
He lost his keys but somehow got into the house.
The team was behind, but they somehow won the game.
I don't know how, but she somehow completed the task on time.
Despite the obstacles, they somehow made it to the top of the mountain.

sense.definition: in some obscure manner or by some unspecified means that one does not know or understand thoroughly
used to suggest that something is assumed to be true, often with a hint of doubt

examples.x: She was supposedly the last person to leave the building, according to eyewitnesses.
The package was supposedly delivered yesterday, but I haven't found it yet.
The new restaurant is supposedly the best in town, according to online reviews.
The treasure map supposedly leads to a hidden island with untold riches.
He supposedly has insider information, but we should verify the facts before making any decisions.

sense.definition: not firmly established or decided, with the possibility of changes in the future

examples.x: They reached a tentative agreement on the terms of the contract, pending further negotiation.
The date for the event is tentative, as we're still waiting to confirm the availability of the venue.
His acceptance of the job offer was tentative, as he wanted to discuss the salary before finalizing anything.
The plan for the project is tentative, with room for adjustments as more information becomes available.
The schedule for the meeting is tentative, depending on the availability of key participants.
The report offered a tentative conclusion, with more research needed to solidify the findings.
The company made a tentative offer to the candidate, pending reference checks.

sense.definition: in a way that is not certain or definite and might be changed later

examples.x: She tentatively agreed to join the team, pending further details.
The meeting is tentatively scheduled for next Monday, but it might change.
He tentatively suggested a new approach, awaiting feedback from the team.
The company tentatively approved the proposal, pending final budget review.
She tentatively started the project, unsure of its feasibility.

sense.definition: ‌used to express that no decision is made or no opinion is formed about something due to uncertainty

word_list.word: there is many a slip (twixt|between) cup and lip

sense.definition: used to imply that even when something seems certain or likely to happen, there are many opportunities for it to go wrong or for unexpected events to intervene before it is actually achieved

examples.x: The team was on the verge of winning the championship, but as they say, there is many a slip twixt cup and lip, and they ultimately lost the game due to a last-minute mistake.

sense.definition: used to convey that something cannot happen under any given circumstances
not definitively known or decided

examples.x: The outcome of the negotiations remains uncertain as both parties are still in disagreement.
The future of the economy is uncertain due to ongoing global instability.
The weather forecast for tomorrow is uncertain, with a chance of rain or sunshine.
The outcome of the experiment is uncertain until all the data has been analyzed.
The date of the event is uncertain due to potential scheduling conflicts.

sense.definition: something about which one cannot be certain

examples.x: The scientist expressed uncertainty about the results of the experiment due to inconsistent data.
The uncertainty surrounding the upcoming election made many investors cautious.
There was a great deal of uncertainty about whether the event would proceed as planned due to the weather.
Her uncertainty about her career path led her to seek advice from a mentor.
The company's future is filled with uncertainty after the sudden resignation of its CEO.

sense.definition: not exactly known or expressed, often leading to confusion or ambiguity

examples.x: It’s still unclear whether the event will be postponed due to the weather.
His intentions were unclear, making it hard to trust his actions completely.
Their future plans remain unclear as they are still weighing their options.
His intentions were unclear, making it hard to trust his actions completely.

sense.definition: having doubts about the validity or credibility of something
having a low chance of happening or being true

examples.x: It's unlikely that it will rain tomorrow, as the weather forecast predicts clear skies.
It's unlikely that she will change her mind about quitting her job; she seems determined.
Winning the lottery is unlikely, given the astronomical odds against it.
It's unlikely that he will arrive on time, considering his habitual tardiness.
Being struck by lightning is unlikely, statistically speaking, but it's still important to take precautions during a thunderstorm.
It's unlikely that they will finish the project on time given the current progress.

sense.definition: having doubts about or no confidence in someone or something

examples.x: He was unsure if he wanted pizza or pasta for dinner.
She felt unsure about taking the new job.
They were unsure where to go on vacation.
The student was unsure about the homework assignment.
She looked unsure when asked to give a speech.

sense.definition: to become less resolved or determined

examples.x: Despite her initial enthusiasm, her resolve began to weaken as the challenges of the project became apparent.
He vowed to quit smoking, but his resolve weakened when he faced stressful situations.
As the weeks passed without progress, their determination to find a solution gradually weakened.
The athlete's resolve never weakened, even in the face of setbacks and injuries.
Despite his initial determination to finish the marathon, his resolve weakened as fatigue set in.

sense.definition: used to express an opinion about which one is not certain

examples.x: She would probably agree with your proposal if you explain it clearly.
I would think that they'll arrive before noon, given the traffic conditions.
They would likely support the initiative if it aligns with their values.
He would hope that his hard work will pay off in the end.

sense.definition: used to say that one can never be sure of something

examples.x: In the ever-changing world of technology, you can never tell which new innovation will become the next big thing.
When it comes to people's behavior, you can never tell how they will react in a given situation.
Weather forecasts are often accurate, but sometimes, you can never tell when a sudden storm might appear.
In the world of art, one can never tell which piece will become a timeless masterpiece.
When it comes to investments, one can never tell for sure how the market will perform in the long run.
